title: when I add old feed in old/previous date which I forgot to add, the i get ale...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

when I add old feed in old/previous date which I forgot to add, the i get alerts that no feed in xx hours. check similar errors and fix it

---

**Category:** `bug` `enhancement` `feeding`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Bug: Adding feeds for past dates triggers false "no feed in XX hours" alerts.

What the user reported: When logging a feed entry for a previous date (e.g., one they forgot to record), the app fires alerts saying no feed in XX hours, as if the baby hasn't been fed recently.

Affected components:
- src/features/insights/SmartStatus.tsx (lines 48-99): calculates hoursAgo from last feed vs Date.now()
- src/features/insights/useDynamicRedFlags.ts (lines 92-123): P0 alert for extended feeding gaps
- src/lib/utils/feedNotification.ts (lines 33-67): native/browser notification for feed gaps
- src/features/insights/PredictiveNudges.tsx (lines 104-135): past-window feeding nudges

Root cause: All alert systems determine the "last feed" by scanning feed entries and comparing timestamps to Date.now(). When a user adds a feed for a past date, the system treats that past-dated entry as the most recent feed, making it appear the baby hasn't been fed for many hours.

Expected behavior: Alerts should calculate gap based on the chronologically most recent feed timestamp, regardless of when the entry was added. If feeds exist for today, adding an older feed to yesterday should not change the alert state.

Edge cases to handle:
- User adds the only feed of the day to a past date (alert should still fire for today if no today feeds exist)
- User edits an existing feed to change its date to the past
- Multiple feeds across dates - ensure sorting is by actual feed date+time, not array insertion order

Acceptance criteria:
- Adding a feed for a past date does not trigger false positive alerts
- Alerts still fire correctly when there genuinely is no recent feed
- All four alert systems (SmartStatus, DynamicRedFlags, feedNotification, PredictiveNudges) handle past-date feeds correctly

Testing: Add unit tests for each alert component verifying:
1. Past-date feed insertion does not trigger alert when recent feeds exist
2. Alerts still fire when no recent feeds exist (only old entries)
3. Feed sorting by actual timestamp works correctly across date boundaries
implementation_notes:
Fixed false alerts when adding feeds for past dates, plus same fix for all similar patterns.

Files changed:
- src/features/insights/SmartStatus.tsx: Added latestEntry() helper. Changed feed, diaper, and sleep status to use chronologically latest entry instead of array[0].
- src/features/insights/useDynamicRedFlags.ts: Added latestEntryMs() and latestEntryOf() helpers. Fixed feed gap, dirty diaper gap, and tummy time gap to find chronologically latest entries.
- src/lib/utils/feedNotification.ts: Changed to scan all feeds for chronologically latest instead of using feeds[0].
- src/features/insights/PredictiveNudges.tsx: Added latestMs() helper. Fixed feed, diaper, and sleep predictions to use chronologically latest entries.

Tests updated:
- tests/unit/dynamicRedFlags.test.ts: Updated local computeRedFlags copy to match source. Added 3 tests for past-date ordering (feed, dirty diaper, mixed order).
- tests/unit/feedNotification.test.ts: Updated existing test to verify chronological ordering. Added test for past-date feed prepended before recent feed.

Root cause: All alert systems assumed array[0] was the chronologically most recent entry. But when users add entries for past dates, they get prepended to the array, making a past-dated entry appear at index 0. The fix scans all entries to find the one with the latest actual date+time.

title: if something needs attention / risk in quick log, it should continue to remai...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

if something needs attention / risk in quick log, it should continue to remain in top 8.

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Bug: Quick log items with attention/risk warnings can be hidden when not in the top 8 visible slots.

What the user reported: If a quick log item needs attention (has a warning or danger indicator), it should always remain visible in the collapsed top-8 view, not get pushed off-screen by non-warning items.

Component affected: src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx — the quick log grid (lines ~1663-1673)

Current behaviour: The topItems logic (line 1669) adds warning/active items past position 8, but then visibleItems = topItems.slice(0, 8) on line 1673 cuts them off. Warning items that appear after the 8th position in the array get hidden.

Expected behaviour: Items with quickLogWarnings (attention/risk) should always be prioritized in the top 8 slots. Non-warning items should yield their position to make room for warning items.

Root cause: The loop builds topItems by appending items in order — first 8 regardless of status, then only warning/active items. But the final slice(0, 8) doesn't account for warning items that were appended after position 8.

Fix approach: Sort/partition items so warning/active items come first, then fill remaining slots (up to 8) with non-warning items. This ensures warnings are always visible in the collapsed view.

Edge cases:
- More than 8 items have warnings — show all 8 as warnings, rest in expanded view
- Active timers (nursing, tummy time, sleep) should also be prioritized
- Expanded view (qlExpanded) should continue to show all items unchanged
- Order within priority groups should remain stable (preserve original qlItems order)

Acceptance criteria:
- Items with quickLogWarnings always appear in the collapsed top-8 grid
- Active/highlighted items also remain visible in top 8
- When collapsed view has room after placing all warning/active items, fill with non-warning items in original order
- Expanded view is unaffected
- No regression in warning threshold logic

Testing: Add unit tests for the top-8 partitioning logic — test that warning items always appear in the visible set even when they would otherwise be in position 9+. Test edge case where >8 items have warnings.
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Refactored quick log top-8 partitioning logic (lines ~1663-1673)

Tests added:
- tests/unit/quickLogPriority.test.ts: 9 tests covering priority partitioning — warning items promoted, active/highlight items promoted, >8 priority items, expanded view, fewer than 8 items, ordering

Approach: Changed from a single-pass append (which added warning items past position 8 then sliced them off) to a two-pass partition: first separate priority items (warning/active) from normal items, then build visible list as priority items first + remaining slots filled with normal items. This guarantees warning items always appear in the collapsed top-8 grid.
